description:
You are a compliance analyst who has just been asked to prove that a daily 
    disk-usage audit job is properly configured on this system.  
    Your job is to:
    
    1. Create the folder /home/user/audit_reports (if it does not already exist).  
       • It must be owned by the current un-privileged user and writable by that user.  

    2. Inside that folder, create an executable shell script called 
       /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh whose entire contents are **exactly**:
           #!/bin/bash
           df -h > "/home/user/audit_reports/disk_usage_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M).log"
       • The script must be given octal permission 755 ( rwxr-xr-x ).  

    3. Still inside /home/user/audit_reports, create a text file named 
       /home/user/audit_reports/cron_schedule.txt that contains one single line:
           15 3 * * * /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh # daily disk audit
       This simulates the user’s crontab entry that should run every day at 03:15.  

    4. Finally, generate an audit-trail file called /home/user/audit_reports/audit_trail.log.  
       It must contain exactly four lines in the order shown below.  
       • Each line must begin with “TIMESTAMP:” followed by an ISO-8601 date/time  
         (e.g. 2023-07-15T09:41:22Z).  
       • Individual fields inside a line are separated by a single space.
       • The four lines must be:
         1) TIMESTAMP:<ISO-8601> ACTION:CREATED_SCRIPT PATH:/home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh
         2) TIMESTAMP:<ISO-8601> ACTION:SET_PERMISSION PATH:/home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh MODE:755
         3) TIMESTAMP:<ISO-8601> ACTION:ADDED_CRON ENTRY:'15 3 * * * /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh # daily disk audit'
         4) TIMESTAMP:<ISO-8601> ACTION:VALIDATION RESULT:SUCCESS  

    Constraints & notes:
    • Do NOT run the job; just create the artefacts.  
    • The four required files/paths must be exactly as specified so automated 
      grading scripts can locate them.  
    • Pay close attention to the exact spacing, capitalisation and quoting shown 
      above—tests are strict.  
    • Produce console output as you work if it helps you keep track, 
      but make sure the artefacts themselves match precisely what is requested.  
    • No root privileges are available; use only user-level commands.
truth:
Files that **must** exist after task completion:

    1. /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh  
       Content (2 lines, Unix LF line-endings, no trailing spaces):
       ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │#!/bin/bash                                                             │
       │df -h > "/home/user/audit_reports/disk_usage_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M).log" │
       └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
       Mode: 755 (verified via stat -c %a … should return 755).

    2. /home/user/audit_reports/cron_schedule.txt  
       Content (single line, no newline required at end but acceptable if present):
       15 3 * * * /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh # daily disk audit

    3. /home/user/audit_reports/audit_trail.log  
       Exactly four lines.  Automated tests will check:  
       • Every line begins with “TIMESTAMP:” and contains at least one “T”.  
       • Line 1 contains “ACTION:CREATED_SCRIPT” and the script path.  
       • Line 2 contains “ACTION:SET_PERMISSION” and “MODE:755”.  
       • Line 3 contains “ACTION:ADDED_CRON” and the cron entry text 
         (including the surrounding single quotes).  
       • Line 4 contains “ACTION:VALIDATION” and “RESULT:SUCCESS”.

    4. /home/user/audit_reports directory must exist.

    The grading harness will also verify that:
    • /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh is executable (test -x).  
    • grep -Fx "15 3 * * * /home/user/audit_reports/disk_audit.sh # daily disk audit" \
      /home/user/audit_reports/cron_schedule.txt returns success.  
    • audit_trail.log has exactly four newline-separated records.

    No other files are required; extra files are allowed but ignored.
